g.data-narration: This topic covers how software projects are evaluated in university courses, including criteria, rubrics, and examples.
Project evaluation assesses student understanding, measures practical coding abilities, and evaluates documentation skills.
The main evaluation dimensions are technical quality, documentation, presentation, and process adherence.
Implementation carries the highest weightage at 30%, while viva voce is 25%, documentation 20%, design 15%, and synopsis 10%.
The evaluation process has four stages: preliminary review, mid-term assessment, final evaluation, and viva voce.
Rubrics provide structured criteria with performance levels: Excellent, Good, Satisfactory, and Poor with specific descriptors.
Quantitative metrics include Lines of Code, cyclomatic complexity, code coverage, and test case effectiveness.
In the Library Management System example, the student scored 84% (First Class) with good implementation but missing unit tests.
The E-Commerce app example shows how security vulnerabilities and missing documentation led to a lower Second Class score.
g.text: ⚠ Key Lesson: Security issues and missing documentation severely impact evaluation!

g.data-narration: This rubric shows scoring criteria for mobile apps with categories like functionality, UI/UX, code quality, and innovation.
Key exam tips include focusing on complete working systems, maintaining version control, and preparing thoroughly for viva voce.
g.text: 1. Implementation has highest weightage - focus on complete working systems
2. Documentation must match implementation exactly - inconsistencies = penalty
3. Maintain proper Git history - single massive commit raises plagiarism suspicion
4. Test presentation on different machines, not just your dev environment
5. Include error handling - code without try-catch is considered incomplete
✓ Prepare for design questions in viva

g.data-narration: Common mistakes include incomplete implementations, copy-paste code, ignoring documentation, and last-minute submissions.
In summary, focus on complete working implementation, accurate documentation, proper version control, and thorough viva preparation.
g.text: ✓ Implementation (30%) carries highest weightage - ensure core features work completely
✓ Documentation must match implementation - verify consistency before submission
✓ Version control proves your work - maintain consistent commit history
